title: I need references for my wikipedia article on RE TAKE
display_title: I need references for my wikipedia article on RE TAKE
wikitext:
[]: I need references for my wikipedia article on RE TAKE             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Wed Oct 15, 2008 10:23 pm http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neon_Genesis_Evangelion_RE-TAKE Some are trying to delete my article for this wonderful series, saying I need to find proper references. I mention that my Google search found it talked about more than 50 thousand times across the net, and surely that was notable enough to have an article. But, alas, some disagree. Are there any websites out there that would have a review about this, which would be considered good reference material by them? I used Google to search for websites written in Japanese, and then read through them with Google translator, but can't find any Doujinshi review publications. Does anyone know of any? It is odd that it could become this popular, by word of mouth alone, and there not be at least one manga review site out there mentioning it. Note, they said that sites that are user submitted databases don't count as references. I noted a very long thread about Re-Take on this site, which is why I came here, looking for fans of the series. For those of you who don't know about it yet, go here http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Neon_Genesis_Evangelion_RE-TAKE and read all about it. Don't hesitate to contribute. I found a live action video some fans made in Japan, which I linked to. I thought it nicely done. Remember, read them in the order 1,2,3,0,4, After. If you read the 0 one first, it'll ruin a few surprises for you, and fill you with great regret you didn't read it in the proper order.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Wed Oct 15, 2008 10:29 pm You are my new best friend for contributing to the world of Re-Take. Re-Take is my favorite doujinshi because it fits, in my opinion, almost perfectly into the Evangelion universe. I would be glad to help you write or add upon the Re-Take Wikipedia article.

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Wed Oct 15, 2008 11:54 pm The thing about the "live action bit of the series" is wrong. They're releasing the whole thing, but in parts. You should also probably mention stuff like the names being changed for copyright purposes. I'd do it myself, but the article's almost surely going to be deleted anyway.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 4:00 am Actually, if just the two of you voted to Keep, that'd be enough to keep it. They have to form a consensus before they can delete it, and I'm not the only one who thinks it should stay. Please log into wikipedia, and post Keep. And is there an English site somewhere listing information about the live action thing? Or is it only at that site I linked to? I was using Google Translator but didn't see any text about it, so if you have a link to the rest of it, please let me know.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:29 am Use Zeak's Re-Take Project http://www.zeak.com.au/ Also I posted in the discussion section under Deletion. I also added to the article, Animated. That guy managing it seems like a jerk. "I don't like it" What a douche. Zeak's project should be enough to have an article by itself.

[]:             Quote Post by The Bastard King » Thu Oct 16, 2008 8:48 am Surely you guys are kidding. Who else do you know that would name themselves "Vi Veri Veniversum Vivus Vici"? I'm sensing a Wikipedia article debate in 5, 4...

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Oct 16, 2008 9:24 am Crap. Like myself, he is a member of the Colbert Nation. So therefor, I can predict no end to this argument. Also I think this is a little weird.... Vi Veri Veniversum Vivus Vici wrote: One of my insane long-term goals is to found "WikiProject FastFood" and a FastFood Portal, basically uniting all articles on McDonalds, Burger King, Taco Bell, Wendy's, etc. into one central hub.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Thu Oct 16, 2008 9:35 am (whips out his secret CN decoder ring) wow, you're a member of the Nation too? :) well I'm sorry Dream and Irken that the article got deleted; but its bigger than me; look how many people supported deleting it and for why: the problem is that you have to "cite a third party revew" and like, ANN never did that. As for popularity, it's not "proof" of "*widespread* popularity" if there are fanvids...***even "Eva-R" didn't get on wikipedia, and Tiffany Grant supported it be of good cheer, all is not lost: you simply have to take this up with the higher-ups by going to "WikiProject Anime and Manga" and asking for the rule about not including UNlicensed fanfiction to be changed. So do that and try again. (I'm not really sure if it will work though, though at this point its out of my hands)

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Oct 16, 2008 11:02 am Welp Dream, I tried. I just think that it's stupid. Wikipedia is an online encyclopedia, I don't see why it would matter what's really in it or not. And no third party or fan made movies? If this wasn't anime it would be perfectly fine to put it on there.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 11:26 am Well, they didn't delete it quite yet. Just got to suggest a rule change then. If its popular, that's the same as being notable, isn't it? And if its notable, then its get a page. And it says using a "Google test" or "search engine test" is a way to prove something is notable. I honestly don't understand what rule we're violating here. I don't see why web comics that are based in the Final Fantasy world, using the same characters in many cases, get their own pages, while something which is basically the same, just not displayed in the same manner, does not. And seriously, why aren't there any Doujinshi review sites out there that could count as a reference? If only someone who spoke Japanese could help do a Google search in that language. Going through Google translator is hard. But as suggested, I'll go and ask for a policy change. If the rules are unfair we must protest them, so that they might be changed.

[]:             Quote Post by BobBQ » Thu Oct 16, 2008 11:46 am You don't need a Wikipedia article on ReTake.

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 11:55 am And why not? Is it not notable? When people hear about something, they usually go to the wikipedia to find out information about it. I see it as no less valid as anything else. http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Wikipedia_talk:WikiProject_Anime_and_manga#Suggest_policy_change.__Can_we_include_unlicensed_fanfiction.2Fdoujinshi.3F Anyway, I posted my suggestions for a policy change. Please look them over, and post your comments there, whether you agree or disagree.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 16, 2008 12:55 pm There's already an EvaPedia article on Re-Take, but adding anything about the fanimation project (and the live-action project I think) are discouraged since it's not Kimigabuchi's actual work and those can be promoted on their own. http://evageeks.org/FGP/Re-Take

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 3:15 pm I agree with the fan made thing not being needed in there. Someone else added it, and the article isn't that long, so I just ignored it. I'm an adder not a deleter. The Re-Take article you linked to is incomplete, but has more stuff about the episodes than whats on the wikipedia site. I wonder if the author would mind letting us copy and paste his work over... Anyway, the "Links" section on the author's official site shows various stores that sell his stuff. These are physical locations in Japan, they also selling things online and through mail order. I added mention to one of these, hoping that'll make it seem more notable. Going to make an article for the author himself, since some of his other work is mentioned in major publications/review sites I believe. Just got to find those pages again and make certain, and that will qualify him as "notable" and thus everything he has done is "notable". That's how these strange rules work, at least to the interpretation of some.

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Thu Oct 16, 2008 3:23 pm Umm, I was personally adding some information to the Evageeks wiki but no you can't add it onto Wikipedia. Doing so would be plagiarism.

[]:             Quote Post by IrkenEvangelion » Thu Oct 16, 2008 3:36 pm I hate to jump off ship on you, Dream, but some people are just ignorant. It's hard convincing other people, especially higher ups, to see your point of view because they do have the power and why should they listen to you? It's just a battle that I think we couldn't win if we wanted to.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 7:38 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: There's already an EvaPedia article on Re-Take

[]:             Quote Post by Dream Focus » Thu Oct 16, 2008 7:45 pm Sailor Star Dust wrote: Umm, I was personally adding some information to the Evageeks wiki but no you can't add it onto Wikipedia. Doing so would be plagiarism.

[]:             Quote Post by Willbyr » Sat Oct 18, 2008 10:47 am DF, I'm glad you tried to fight for this article, but the thing that you're confusing is that, for Wikipedia's purposes, "popular" doesn't mean "notable." For something to be considered notable, it has to have had some notice by a major media that's not directly connected to the item in question, such as a magazine article, newspaper, or book. Say for instance, it was mentioned on a manga/anime show that's broadcast on a TV station in Japan, or it received a review in a magazine such as Newtype. These would be valid references, and establish notability. Hell, even if you'd been able to find a discussion in a source like this about legal issues regarding doujinshi that mentioned RE-TAKE, this could've been used. Unfortunately, this wasn't the case. You also have to keep in mind, Wikipedia is very concerned about copyright issues, which is why it shies away from any association with works that aren't officially licensed by an established company, which RE-TAKE definitely isn't. Hopefully Gwern was able to have a copy of the article saved to his userspace as he requested. Maybe at some point in the future, something can be done with it that will make it a valid article, but right now, such isn't the case.

[]:             Quote Post by V » Sat Oct 18, 2008 1:37 pm Yes, the folks on the anime and manga project rules section will take over the discussion but I really dont' think they'll let in anything about it

I mention that my Google search found it talked about more than 50 thousand times across the net, and surely that was notable enough to have an article. B

Article: I need references for my wikipedia article on RE TAKE
 you're confusing is that, for Wikipedia's purposes, "popular" doesn't mean "notable." For something to be considered notable, it has to have had some notice by a major media that's not directly connected to the item in question, such as a magazine article, newspaper, or book. Say for instance, it was mentioned on a manga/anime show that's broadcast on a TV station in Japan, or it received a review in a magazine such as Newtype. These would be valid references, and establish notability. Hell, even if you'd been able to find a discussion in a source like this about legal issues regarding doujinshi that mentioned RE-TAKE, this could've been used. Unfortunately, this wasn't the case. You also have to keep in mind, Wikipedia is very concerned about copyright issues, which is why it shies away from any association with works that aren't officially licensed by an established company, which RE-TAKE definitely isn't. Hopefully Gwern was able to have a copy of the article saved to his userspace as he requested. Maybe at some point in the future, something can be done with it that will make it a valid article, but right now, such isn't the case.
